old_claim.note: Artificial Intelligence (Early) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: Artificial Intelligence (Early) is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

source_shape.source_locator: The DENDRAL history report explicitly places expert systems within the 1960s early AI program and frames them as descendants of early AI methods.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source provides a primary account of DENDRAL as one of the earliest practical expert systems in artificial intelligence.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The report anchors expert systems to early AI as an antecedent line, justifying a stronger lineage evidence level for the dependency and its historical scope.

ontology_before: The edge was supported only as expert-inferred lineage between historical labels.
ontology_after: The same dependency now carries primary-source evidence-level support.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: dependency remains Historical AI lineage. Changed: evidence strength improved from inference to primary source support.
would_reject_if: The DENDRAL source were found to be a secondary summary rather than primary evidence for the broader Artificial Intelligence (Early) node.
short_reason: A primary historical account now directly supports this lineage edge.
